message:

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"caam-jdrl","title":"Implement token aggregation and daily rollup","description":"# Task: Implement token aggregation and daily rollup

## Overview
Create functions to aggregate LogEntry data into daily/weekly/monthly summaries with model breakdowns.

## Technical Details

### Aggregation Functions
```go
// Aggregate aggregates log entries into a TokenUsage summary
func Aggregate(entries []*LogEntry) *TokenUsage

// AggregateByDay groups entries by date and aggregates each day
func AggregateByDay(entries []*LogEntry) []*DailyUsage

// AggregateByModel groups entries by model
func AggregateByModel(entries []*LogEntry) map[string]*ModelTokenUsage
```

### DailyUsage Structure
```go
type DailyUsage struct {
    Date     string       // "2025-01-10"
    Usage    *TokenUsage
    ByModel  map[string]*ModelTokenUsage
}
```

## Dependencies
- Create internal/logs package structure (caam-xz27)
- Implement Claude JSONL log scanner (caam-ivxt)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:17:28.550989733-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T16:52:47.61503251-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T16:52:47.61503251-05:00","close_reason":"Token aggregation functions fully implemented in internal/logs/types.go.  {"id":"caam-je9o","title":"E2E: Monitor and precheck commands with all output formats","description":"# Task: E2E Tests for Monitor and Precheck Commands

## Overview
End-to-end tests for the caam monitor and caam precheck commands with all output formats.

## Test Categories

### 1. Monitor Command Tests

#### Table Format
```go
func TestMonitorTableFormat(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam monitor --format table --once")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "monitor", "--format", "table", "--once")
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Output length: %d bytes", len(output))
    t.Logf("[TEST] First 500 chars:\n%s", output[:min(500, len(output))])
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    require.Contains(t, output, "LIVE USAGE MONITOR")
    require.Contains(t, output, "CLAUDE")
}
```

#### Brief Format (tmux integration)
```go
func TestMonitorBriefFormat(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam monitor --format brief --once")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "monitor", "--format", "brief", "--once")
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Output: %q", output)
    t.Logf("[TEST] Length: %d chars", len(output))
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    require.Less(t, len(output), 80, "Brief format should fit in status bar")
    require.Regexp(t, `\w+:\d+%`, output) // e.g., "claude:42%"
}
```

#### JSON Format
```go
func TestMonitorJSONFormat(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam monitor --format json --once")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "monitor", "--format", "json", "--once")
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] JSON output:\n%s", output)
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    var result map[string]interface{}
    err = json.Unmarshal([]byte(output), &result)
    t.Logf("[TEST] Parse result: err=%v", err)
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    require.Contains(t, result, "updated_at")
    require.Contains(t, result, "profiles")
}
```

### 2. Precheck Command Tests

#### Full Precheck Output
```go
func TestPrecheckFull(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam precheck claude")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "precheck", "claude")
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Output:\n%s", output)
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    require.Contains(t, output, "SESSION PLANNER")
    require.Contains(t, output, "RECOMMENDED")
}
```

#### Brief Format
```go
func TestPrecheckBrief(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam precheck claude --format brief")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "precheck", "claude", "--format", "brief")
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Brief output: %q", output)
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    // Should be a one-liner recommendation
}
```

#### JSON Format
```go
func TestPrecheckJSON(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam precheck claude --format json")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "precheck", "claude", "--format", "json")
    
    var result map[string]interface{}
    json.Unmarshal([]byte(output), &result)
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Keys: %v", keys(result))
    
    require.Contains(t, result, "recommended")
    require.Contains(t, result, "backups")
    require.Contains(t, result, "forecast")
}
```

### 3. Error Handling Tests
- No profiles configured
- Provider not supported
- Network errors (mock)
- Partial failures (some profiles error)

### 4. Integration Tests
- Monitor + jq piping
- Precheck + shell scripting
- Output to file

## Test Infrastructure

### Mock Server for Rate Limits
```go
func setupMockRateLimitServer(t *testing.T) *httptest.Server {
    return httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
        t.Logf("[TEST] Mock request: %s %s", r.Method, r.URL.Path)
        // Return mock rate limit data
    }))
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log exact command being run
- Log full stdout/stderr
- Log exit code
- Log any parsing errors
- Log timing of operations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All output formats work correctly
- [ ] Monitor --once exits cleanly
- [ ] Precheck shows all sections
- [ ] JSON output is valid and parseable
- [ ] Brief output fits in 80 chars
- [ ] Errors are handled gracefully
- [ ] Tests work with mock data

## Files to Create
- cmd/caam/cmd/monitor_e2e_test.go
- cmd/caam/cmd/precheck_e2e_test.go
- cmd/caam/cmd/testutil_test.go

 {"id":"caam-jig","title":"Headless Authentication Support (Device Code Flow)","description":"# Headless Server Support

## Status: Core Features Complete!

The primary headless workflows are now fully functional:

### ✅ Completed

| Feature | Status |
|---------|--------|
| Device auth setup | Works via native \`codex login --device-auth\` + \`caam backup\` |
| Hit-limit → switch → resume | Works via \`caam activate\` + native \`codex resume\` |
| Vault transfer | ✅ \`caam export/import\` implemented (caam-zha) |
| Documentation | ✅ AGENTS.md updated (caam-1r6) |
| AuthModeDeviceCode constant | ✅ Added (caam-hzu) |

### Remaining (P2/P3 - Profile Isolation Mode Only)

These are for the **advanced** Profile Isolation mode, not the primary workflow:

| ID | Task | Priority | Purpose |
|----|------|----------|---------|
| caam-v89 | --device-code CLI flag | P2 | For \`caam login\` in Profile Isolation mode |
| caam-ela | Codex provider support | P2 | Implementation for above |
| caam-0gy | Session resume | P3 | For Profile Isolation mode only |

## The Working Workflow

```bash
# SETUP (on headless server)
codex login --device-auth    # Native Codex
caam backup codex account1   # Existing caam

# HIT LIMIT → SWITCH → RESUME
caam activate codex account2             # < 1 second!
codex resume 019b2e3d-... "proceed"      # Native Codex
```

## Closed Tasks

| ID | Reason |
|----|--------|
| caam-1xv | Research complete - Claude lacks good headless option |
| caam-ai6 | Research complete - vault transfer is best for Gemini |
| caam-e79 | Removed - headless detection was over-engineering |
| caam-m5a | Removed - QR code was gold-plating |","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:36:00.94446079-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:40:21.386555184-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:40:21.386555184-05:00","close_reason":"Core headless workflows complete: device auth setup, hit-limit→switch→resume, vault transfer, and documentation. Remaining tasks (caam-v89, caam-ela) are P2 enhancements for Profile Isolation mode."}
-{"id":"caam-jjoi","title":"Create internal/monitor package with state management","description":"# Task: Create internal/monitor package with state management

## Overview
Create the core monitor package that manages state for real-time usage monitoring across all profiles.

## Technical Details

### State Structures
```go
// internal/monitor/monitor.go

type Monitor struct {
    interval    time.Duration
    providers   []string
    
    fetcher     *usage.MultiProfileFetcher
    vault       *authfile.Vault
    healthStore *health.Storage
    db          *db.DB
    
    state       *MonitorState
    mu          sync.RWMutex
}

type MonitorState struct {
    Profiles  map[string]*ProfileState  // "provider/name" -> state
    UpdatedAt time.Time
    Errors    []string
}

type ProfileState struct {
    Provider      string
    ProfileName   string
    Usage         *usage.UsageInfo
    Health        health.HealthStatus
    PoolStatus    authpool.PoolStatus
    InCooldown    bool
    CooldownUntil *time.Time
    Alert         *Alert
}

type Alert struct {
    Type    AlertType
    Message string
    Since   time.Time
}

type AlertType int
const (
    AlertNone AlertType = iota
    AlertWarning    // 70-85%
    AlertCritical   // 85-95%
    AlertExhausted  // 95-100%
)
```

### Core Methods
```go
func NewMonitor(opts ...MonitorOption) *Monitor
func (m *Monitor) Start(ctx context.Context) error  // Blocking loop
func (m *Monitor) Refresh(ctx context.Context) error  // One-shot refresh
func (m *Monitor) GetState() *MonitorState
func (m *Monitor) GetProfile(provider, name string) *ProfileState
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Thread-safe state management
- [ ] Concurrent fetching across all profiles
- [ ] Proper error handling (partial failures dont break monitor)
- [ ] Efficient memory usage for long-running sessions
- [ ] Configurable refresh intervals

## Files to Create
- internal/monitor/monitor.go
- internal/monitor/state.go
- internal/monitor/alerts.go
- internal/monitor/monitor_test.go

 {"id":"caam-jwgx","title":"EPIC: Proactive User Communication - Desktop notifications for token expiry, rate limits, and background operations. The daemon runs silently; users need alerts when action is required. See docs/FEATURE_PLAN_2025Q1.md","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T23:30:57.921267635-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T00:12:54.505236475-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T00:12:54.505236475-05:00","close_reason":"EPIC complete: Implemented CLI warnings for proactive user communication via Alternative 1 approach - simpler than desktop notifications, no external dependencies"}
 {"id":"caam-jwgx.1","title":"Desktop Notifications for Token Events","description":"## Overview
System notifications for token expiry, rate limits, and background operations.

## ⚠️ Priority Note: P3 (Backlog)
Desktop notifications add significant complexity:
- External dependency (beeep or similar library)
- Cross-platform testing (macOS, Linux, Windows)
- Daemon integration
- Notification throttling logic
- User preference management

Consider this a polish feature after core functionality is solid.

## Problem Statement
The daemon runs in background doing token refresh and health checks, but has no way to alert users when something needs attention.

## Alternative Approaches Worth Considering

### Alternative 1: CLI Warning on Next Run
Instead of push notifications, show warnings when user runs any caam command:
```
$ caam ls
⚠️ Warning: Claude profile 'work' token expires in 2 hours
   Run: caam refresh claude work
```
This is simpler and doesn't require external dependencies.

### Alternative 2: Status File
Write a status file the user's shell prompt can check:
```
~/.local/share/caam/status.json
```
Shell integration can show alerts in prompt. Already have shell integration from caam-k9z.

### Alternative 3: True Desktop Notifications
Full beeep integration as originally planned.

## If Implementing Full Notifications

### Design Decisions
- Use `beeep` library for cross-platform support
- Config controls notification levels:
```yaml
notifications:
  enabled: true
  levels:
    critical: true    # Token expired, auth failure
    warning: true     # Token expiring soon, high penalty
    info: false       # Sync complete, backup complete
```
- Rate limit notifications (max 1 per minute per type)
- Respect system DND/Focus modes where possible

### Implementation Tasks
1. Add notifications package using beeep
2. Add NotificationConfig to config package
3. Integrate with daemon for token expiry checks
4. Integrate with wrap for rate limit events
5. Add `caam notify test` command
6. Add notification throttling

## Recommendation
Start with Alternative 1 (CLI warnings) which provides most value with minimal complexity. Only pursue full desktop notifications if users explicitly request it.

## Files to Create/Modify (if full implementation)
- internal/notifications/notify.go - New package
- internal/config/notifications.go - Config
- internal/daemon/daemon.go - Integration
- internal/wrap/wrap.go - Integration
- cmd/caam/cmd/notify.go - Test command","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"BlackMountain","created_at":"2025-12-19T23:38:35.696110786-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T00:12:44.038649218-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T00:12:44.038649218-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Alternative 1 (CLI warnings): created internal/warnings package, integrated into root.go PersistentPreRunE, shows warnings for expiring tokens when running commands (excludes version, paths, validate, doctor, JSON mode, non-terminal)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-jwgx.1","depends_on_id":"caam-jwgx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T23:38:35.697253465-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"caam-k9z","title":"Shell integration: prompt, completion, auto-activate","description":"## Overview
Deep shell integration that makes caam invisible during daily use. Users type familiar commands (claude, codex) and caam handles everything behind the scenes.

## The Problem
Users must consciously use caam commands:
```bash
caam activate claude work
claude "help me"
# ... later ...
caam next claude
claude "continue"
```

This requires:
- Remembering to use caam
- Knowing current profile state
- Manual switching

## The Solution
Shell integration with three components:

### 1. Smart Aliases (caam shell-init)
```bash
# User adds to .bashrc/.zshrc:
eval "$(caam shell-init)"

# This creates functions that wrap the AI CLIs:
# claude() { caam run claude "$@"; }
# codex() { caam run codex "$@"; }
# gemini() { caam run gemini "$@"; }
```

Now `claude "prompt"` automatically:
- Uses the best available profile
- Handles rate limits transparently
- Records usage for analytics

### 2. Tab Completion
```bash
caam activate cl<TAB>     → claude
caam activate claude w<TAB> → work-account-1
caam switch <TAB>         → [shows all profiles with health]
```

Uses Cobra's built-in completion + dynamic profile completion.

### 3. Prompt Integration (Optional)
Show current profile in shell prompt:
```bash
# In PS1/PROMPT:
alice@work ~/code (claude:work-1) $

# Or as right-prompt showing all:
                                [claude:work codex:main]
```

### 4. Auto-Activation (direnv-style)
```bash
cd ~/work/backend
# caam: Activated workspace 'work' for this directory
#       claude: work-account
#       codex: work-codex

cd ~/personal/side-project  
# caam: Activated workspace 'personal'
#       claude: personal
```

## Technical Design

### Shell Init Command
`caam shell-init [bash|zsh|fish]` outputs shell-specific code:

**Bash/Zsh:**
```bash
# Wrapper functions
claude() { caam run claude "$@"; }
codex() { caam run codex "$@"; }
gemini() { caam run gemini "$@"; }

# Completion
eval "$(caam completion bash)"

# Prompt helper (optional)
_caam_prompt() {
  caam status --format=prompt 2>/dev/null
}

# Auto-activation hook (optional)
_caam_chpwd() {
  caam project check --quiet
}
chpwd_functions+=(_caam_chpwd)  # zsh
# or PROMPT_COMMAND for bash
```

**Fish:**
```fish
function claude; caam run claude $argv; end
function codex; caam run codex $argv; end
# etc.
```

### Dynamic Completion
Profile names must be completed dynamically:
```go
// In cmd/caam/cmd/activate.go
ValidArgsFunction: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string, toComplete string) ([]string, cobra.ShellCompDirective) {
    if len(args) == 0 {
        // Complete tool names
        return []string{"claude", "codex", "gemini"}, cobra.ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp
    }
    // Complete profile names for the given tool
    profiles := vault.List(args[0])
    return profiles, cobra.ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp
}
```

### Prompt Status
`caam status --format=prompt` returns minimal string:
```
claude:work codex:main
# or with health indicators:
claude:work(🟢) codex:main(🟡)
```

### Auto-Activation Hook
When directory changes:
1. Check if current dir (or parents) have `.caam/` config
2. If workspace defined, activate it
3. If profile mappings defined, activate per-tool
4. Cache result to avoid repeated lookups

## Configuration
```yaml
shell:
  auto_aliases: true           # Create wrapper functions
  completion: true             # Enable tab completion
  prompt_integration: false    # Show in prompt (opt-in)
  auto_activate: true          # direnv-style activation
  
  # Prompt format
  prompt_format: "caam:%t:%p"  # %t=tool, %p=profile
  prompt_show_health: true     # Include health indicator
```

## User Experience

### Installation
```bash
$ caam shell-init --install
Adding to ~/.zshrc:
  eval "$(caam shell-init zsh)"

Restart your shell or run:
  source ~/.zshrc

Done! Now 'claude', 'codex', 'gemini' will use caam automatically.
```

### Daily Use
```bash
$ claude "fix this bug"
# Just works - caam picks best profile, handles limits

$ cd ~/work/backend
# caam: Using workspace 'work'

$ claude "continue the refactor"
# Uses work profile automatically
```

### Completion Demo
```bash
$ caam activate <TAB>
claude    codex    gemini

$ caam activate claude <TAB>
work-1         (🟢 active, expires 23h)
work-2         (🟡 expires 2h)
personal       (🔴 in cooldown)
```

## Implementation Tasks
1. **Shell init command** (cmd/caam/cmd/shell_init.go)
   - Generate shell-specific init code
   - Support bash, zsh, fish
   - --install flag for auto-add to rc file

2. **Dynamic completion** (cmd/caam/cmd/completion.go)
   - Profile name completion
   - Health indicators in completion descriptions
   - Tool name completion

3. **Prompt helper** (cmd/caam/cmd/status.go)
   - --format=prompt flag
   - Minimal output for PS1

4. **Auto-activation hook** (cmd/caam/cmd/project.go)
   - project check --quiet command
   - Workspace activation logic

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `eval "$(caam shell-init)"` works for bash/zsh/fish
- [ ] Tab completion works for all commands
- [ ] Profile names complete with health hints
- [ ] Prompt integration is optional but works
- [ ] Auto-activation triggers on cd (zsh chpwd, bash PROMPT_COMMAND)
- [ ] Performance: shell init adds <10ms to shell startup

## Dependencies
- Cobra completion (built-in)
- Wrapper mode (caam-q9f) for the aliases to work

## Priority: P1 (High Value)
This turns caam into an invisible layer, which is the goal of Zero-Friction UX.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-19T18:59:02.491764838-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T20:51:05.916678255-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T20:51:05.916678255-05:00","close_reason":"Shell integration implemented: caam shell init command generates bash/zsh/fish wrapper functions and tab completions. Users can now set up transparent AI CLI wrapping with eval "$(caam shell init)".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-k9z","depends_on_id":"caam-q9f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T19:10:30.578299863-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
